prompt:
Classify the sentiment of this review as positive, negative, or neutral. Answer with one word.

"Arrived two days early and works exactly as described."

Classify the sentiment as positive, negative, or neutral. One word only.

"Oh great, another update that breaks everything. Fantastic work as always."

Is this email spam or not spam? Answer 'spam' or 'not spam'.

"URGENT: Your account will be suspended! Click here within 24 hours to verify your SSN and avoid fees!!!"

Classify this support ticket's primary intent as one of: refund, bug_report, account_access, other. Answer with the label only.

Hi, I was charged $49.99 twice on March 3rd for my Pro subscription (invoice INV-20331 and INV-20349). I only have one account, registered under maya.chen@example.com. Please refund the duplicate. Also, while you're at it, the mobile app keeps logging me out — this started after the last update. Order ref: ORD-77812. My phone is a Pixel 8.

Label the topic of this headline as sports, politics, technology, or entertainment. One word.

"Chipmaker unveils 2nm process node, claims 30% power reduction"

Triage priority for this alert: answer P1, P2, or P3 only.

"Production database primary unreachable for 4 minutes; failover did not trigger; all writes failing."

Does the following statement express certainty or uncertainty? Answer 'certainty' or 'uncertainty'.

"The results might suggest some improvement, though it's hard to say definitively."

Classify this code review comment as blocking or non-blocking. One word.

"nit: prefer f-strings here, but fine either way"

From the incident timeline, extract the build number that was deployed. Reply with the number only.

Timeline: 09:02 deploy of build 4811 to us-east. 09:14 p95 latency on /checkout rises from 180ms to 2.4s. 09:21 error rate hits 11% (connection pool exhaustion in payments-svc). 09:25 rollback initiated; 09:31 rollback complete but error rate stays at 9%. 09:40 engineer notices payments-svc replicas pinned at max connections to the new read replica provisioned yesterday; replica has wrong instance class. 09:55 traffic shifted to primary, error rate normal by 10:02. Customer impact: ~38k failed checkouts.

From the incident timeline, how many checkouts failed? Reply with the number only.

Timeline: 09:02 deploy of build 4811 to us-east. 09:14 p95 latency on /checkout rises from 180ms to 2.4s. 09:21 error rate hits 11% (connection pool exhaustion in payments-svc). 09:25 rollback initiated; 09:31 rollback complete but error rate stays at 9%. 09:40 engineer notices payments-svc replicas pinned at max connections to the new read replica provisioned yesterday; replica has wrong instance class. 09:55 traffic shifted to primary, error rate normal by 10:02. Customer impact: ~38k failed checkouts.

Translate this legal clause into Portuguese, preserving precise legal meaning:

Section 7.2. Indemnification. Each party (the "Indemnifying Party") shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless the other party from and against any and all third-party claims arising out of (a) the Indemnifying Party's gross negligence or willful misconduct, (b) its breach of Section 5 (Confidentiality), or (c) infringement of intellectual property rights by materials furnished by the Indemnifying Party, except to the extent such claims result from the other party's modifications thereof or combination with materials not furnished by the Indemnifying Party. The foregoing states the Indemnifying Party's entire liability for third-party claims, provided that nothing herein limits liability for amounts payable under Section 9.3 or claims that cannot be limited under applicable law. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the aggregate liability under this Section 7.2 shall not exceed two times the fees paid or payable in the twelve months preceding the claim, except for clauses (b) and (c), which are uncapped.
What is the capital of Australia? One word.
How many bytes are in a kibibyte? Reply with the number only.
What HTTP status code means 'Too Many Requests'? Number only.
What does the acronym CTE stand for in SQL? Short answer.
Which planet has the shortest day? One word.
What year was the original Bretton Woods agreement signed? Year only.

Summarize this incident in two sentences for a status page (no internal details):

Timeline: 09:02 deploy of build 4811 to us-east. 09:14 p95 latency on /checkout rises from 180ms to 2.4s. 09:21 error rate hits 11% (connection pool exhaustion in payments-svc). 09:25 rollback initiated; 09:31 rollback complete but error rate stays at 9%. 09:40 engineer notices payments-svc replicas pinned at max connections to the new read replica provisioned yesterday; replica has wrong instance class. 09:55 traffic shifted to primary, error rate normal by 10:02. Customer impact: ~38k failed checkouts.

Also, while you're at it, the mobile app keeps logging me out — this started after the last update. Order ref: ORD-77812. My phone is a Pixel 8.

Summarize in one sentence:

"After three quarters of declining engagement, the team shipped a redesigned onboarding flow in February. Activation rose 14% month-over-month, retention at day 30 improved from 21% to 26%, and support tickets about setup dropped by a third."

Give me the key takeaways from this standup note as 3 bullets:

"Auth migration is 80% done, remaining work is the SSO edge cases. Marketing site launch slipped to Thursday because legal is still reviewing the testimonials page. The flaky payments test was traced to a timezone assumption and is fixed."

Section 7.2. Indemnification. Each party (the "Indemnifying Party") shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless the other party from and against any and all third-party claims arising out of (a) the Indemnifying Party's gross negligence or willful misconduct, (b) its breach of Section 5 (Confidentiality), or (c) infringement of intellectual property rights by materials furnished by the Indemnifying Party, except to the extent such claims result from the other party's modifications thereof or combination with materials not furnished by the Indemnifying Party. The foregoing states the Indemnifying Party's entire liability for third-party claims, provided that nothing herein limits liability for amounts payable under Section 9.3 or claims that cannot be limited under applicable law. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the aggregate liability under this Section 7.2 shall not exceed two times the fees paid or payable in the twelve months preceding the claim, except for clauses (b) and (c), which are uncapped.

Section 7.2. Indemnification. Each party (the "Indemnifying Party") shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless the other party from and against any and all third-party claims arising out of (a) the Indemnifying Party's gross negligence or willful misconduct, (b) its breach of Section 5 (Confidentiality), or (c) infringement of intellectual property rights by materials furnished by the Indemnifying Party, except to the extent such claims result from the other party's modifications thereof or combination with materials not furnished by the Indemnifying Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the aggregate liability under this Section 7.2 shall not exceed two times the fees paid or payable in the twelve months preceding the claim, except for clauses (b) and (c), which are uncapped.

Write a root-cause summary of this incident for the postmortem doc, distinguishing trigger, root cause, and aggravating factors:

Timeline: 09:02 deploy of build 4811 to us-east. 09:14 p95 latency on /checkout rises from 180ms to 2.4s. 09:21 error rate hits 11% (connection pool exhaustion in payments-svc). 09:25 rollback initiated; 09:31 rollback complete but error rate stays at 9%. 09:40 engineer notices payments-svc replicas pinned at max connections to the new read replica provisioned yesterday; replica has wrong instance class. 09:55 traffic shifted to primary, error rate normal by 10:02. Customer impact: ~38k failed checkouts.

Fix the grammar without changing the meaning:

"Me and the team has went over the numbers and they wasn't matching what finance gave us."

Rewrite this to be more formal for an enterprise customer:

"hey! so yeah the thing broke again lol but we patched it, should be good now"

Convert this prose into a numbered step list:

"First open settings, then go to billing, after that click payment methods and add your new card, finally remove the old one."

Convert this CSV row into a JSON object with keys name, role, start_date:

Priya Sharma,Staff Engineer,2024-06-17

Make this tweet fit in 140 characters without losing the announcement:

"We are absolutely thrilled and delighted to finally announce that our long-awaited dark mode feature is now generally available to all users on every plan starting today!"
Write a Python function that returns the n-th Fibonacci number iteratively.
Write a regex that matches a US ZIP code (5 digits, optional -4 extension). Show the pattern and one usage example in Python.
Write a SQL query that returns the 5 customers with the highest total order value from tables customers(id, name) and orders(id, customer_id, amount).
Write a bash one-liner that finds all .log files over 100MB under /var and prints their sizes, largest first.
Write a Python function `dedupe_preserve_order(items)` that removes duplicates from a list while preserving first-seen order.

Design and implement a Python rate limiter supporting both a token-bucket and a sliding-window strategy behind one interface, with thread safety and tests. Explain the trade-offs between the two strategies.

Refactor a synchronous Flask + requests order-processing service into an async FastAPI + httpx design. Describe the migration plan (what changes, in what order, how to keep both running during cutover), then write the new order endpoint including retry logic and idempotency keys.

Implement an LRU cache with TTL expiry in Python from scratch (no functools.lru_cache): O(1) get/put, lazy expiry, a background sweep, and instrumentation hooks for hit/miss metrics. Include unit tests covering the TTL edge cases.

Design a schema and write the migrations for a multi-tenant SaaS billing system: plans, subscriptions, usage-based add-ons, proration on mid-cycle upgrades, and an audit trail. Postgres. Explain the proration approach and its edge cases.

Based on this incident timeline, what was the root cause, and why did the rollback not fix it? What single change would have prevented the incident?

Timeline: 09:02 deploy of build 4811 to us-east. 09:14 p95 latency on /checkout rises from 180ms to 2.4s. 09:21 error rate hits 11% (connection pool exhaustion in payments-svc). 09:25 rollback initiated; 09:31 rollback complete but error rate stays at 9%. 09:40 engineer notices payments-svc replicas pinned at max connections to the new read replica provisioned yesterday; replica has wrong instance class. 09:55 traffic shifted to primary, error rate normal by 10:02. Customer impact: ~38k failed checkouts.

A nightly batch job's memory usage grows ~200MB per hour until OOM, but heap profiling shows stable Python object counts. The job reads Parquet files with pandas in a loop, processes, writes results. What are the most likely causes and how would you confirm each?

Why does this test pass locally but deadlock in CI?

```python
def test_concurrent_update(db_pool):
    with db_pool.connection() as a, db_pool.connection() as b:
        a.execute('UPDATE accounts SET bal=bal-10 WHERE id=1')
        b.execute('UPDATE accounts SET bal=bal+10 WHERE id=2')
        b.execute('UPDATE accounts SET bal=bal-5 WHERE id=1')
        a.execute('UPDATE accounts SET bal=bal+5 WHERE id=2')
```
CI runs with a 2-connection pool; locally the pool is 10.

A SaaS plan costs $40/seat/month billed annually with a 20% discount, or monthly with none. A team of 13 wants the cheaper option for 9 months of expected use. Which is cheaper and by how much? Show the arithmetic.
Prove that the sum of two even integers is even.
Prove or disprove: if a relation R on a finite set is symmetric and transitive, it must be reflexive. If false, give the smallest counterexample.
An A/B test shows 4.1% conversion (n=12,400) vs 4.6% (n=12,150). Is the difference statistically significant at the 95% level? Walk through the two-proportion z-test and state the conclusion.
You have 9 visually identical coins; one is heavier. What is the minimum number of balance-scale weighings to guarantee finding it, and why is fewer impossible?

Plan the steps to migrate 40 cron jobs from a single VM into containerized scheduled tasks: inventory, dependency mapping, secrets handling, cutover order, rollback strategy, and how you'd verify each job post-migration. Produce a concrete checklist an engineer can execute.

You manage a release across three services with interdependent API changes. Service A's new endpoint must exist before B can deploy; C reads B's events. Design the deployment sequence with feature flags, the verification at each stage, and the abort criteria.

Draft a runbook for on-call: 'checkout error rate above 5%'. Include triage queries, decision tree (deploy? dependency? data?), mitigation actions in order of blast radius, and when to page payments engineering.

We can build an in-house feature-flag system (~2 eng-months, then maintenance) or pay a vendor $30k/yr. 15-person engineering team, Series A, compliance is not a factor. Analyze the trade-offs and recommend, including the hidden costs each side tends to ignore.

Our usage-based pricing causes bill shock; 8% of churned customers cite it. Sales wants seat-based pricing; finance worries about revenue from heavy users. Lay out the options (caps, committed-use discounts, hybrid), their revenue and churn risks, and a sequencing recommendation.

You're advising the customer in this ticket's company. Their support volume doubled in a quarter while headcount is flat. Using the ticket as an example, analyze whether better self-serve flows, AI deflection, or process changes would help most, and what you'd measure to decide:

Hi, I was charged $49.99 twice on March 3rd for my Pro subscription (invoice INV-20331 and INV-20349). I only have one account, registered under maya.chen@example.com. Please refund the duplicate. Also, while you're at it, the mobile app keeps logging me out — this started after the last update. Order ref: ORD-77812. My phone is a Pixel 8.
Write a 250-word launch announcement for a developer tool that turns OpenAPI specs into typed client SDKs. Audience: backend engineers. Tone: confident, concrete, no hype words like 'revolutionary'.
Write a short story (under 400 words) about a lighthouse keeper who receives a letter addressed to someone who never existed. End on an image, not an explanation.
Write the opening section of a blog post titled 'The hidden cost of defaulting to your biggest model', aimed at engineering leaders. Hook with a concrete scenario, 150-200 words.

I have two job offers: a staff role at a stable company for more money, and a senior role at a risky startup with equity. I'm 29, no dependents, some savings. Help me think through how to decide — ask me the questions that actually matter.
Help me brainstorm 10 names for an internal tool that routes AI requests to cheaper models. Mix of serious and playful.

My standup updates ramble. Coach me: rewrite this update and tell me the pattern to follow:

"So yesterday I was looking at the thing with the cache and it turned out to be more complicated because of the invalidation stuff, anyway I also reviewed two PRs and today more of the same probably."
